news.headline: Interfor reports Q4'24 results: Net loss of $49.9M, Adjusted EBITDA $80.4M, sales $746.5M. Lumber prices up. Debt $861.3M.
news.summary: In Q4 2024, Interfor Corporation reported a Net Loss of $49.9 million, compared to $105.7 million in Q3 and $169 million in Q4 2023. Adjusted EBITDA was $80.4 million, showing improvement over the past quarters. Lumber prices rose, with an average selling price of $659 per mfbm. The company benefitted from improved financial position, with net debt at $861.3 million and positive operating cash flow of $74.8 million. Interfor also sold Coastal B.C. forest tenures for $11.6 million. The company's outlook projects market volatility due to economic shifts. Key figures include $746.5 million in total sales and $80 million in Adjusted EBITDA. Major investments are estimated at around $85 million for 2025. Softwood lumber duties expense was $3.1 million in the quarter, with cumulative duties paid at $593.6 million. The company completed the sale of Quebec operations for $16.3 million in net cash. Interfor's strategy involves maintaining a diversified portfolio to mitigate risks. The financial highlights include total assets at $3,078.7 million and net debt-to-invested capital at 36%. The company has enough liquidity and working capital to fund future operations.

news.headline: Definity Financial Corp. reports strong Q4 2024 results with 7.4% premium growth, $110.4M operating net income, and $1.1B GWP. Operating ROE at 10.6%. Book value per share increased by 17.6% to $29.13. Dividend raised by 17%. Personal insurance GWP up by 10.1% in 2024.
news.summary: Definity Financial Group (TSX:DFY) reported a 7.4% growth in gross written premium in Q4 2024, reaching $1,109.5 million, and a 11.1% growth for the full year, hitting $4,448.1 million. The combined ratio in Q4 was 90.3%, showing strength in personal property, with a full-year ratio of 94.5%. Operating net income for Q4 was $110.4 million, delivering an operating EPS of $0.95, and a book value per share increase of 17.6% to $29.13. Net income attributable to common shareholders was $116.6 million in Q4. Personal lines GWP saw a 5.7% increase in Q4 and a 10.1% increase for the year, while commercial lines GWP increased by 10.7% in Q4 and 13.1% for the full year. Underwriting income in Q4 was $97.0 million, net investment income rose by $1.7 million in Q4 and $18.7 million for the year, and distribution income reached $11.4 million in Q4. Operating ROE improved to 10.6% in 2024. Auto gross written premiums increased by 5.5% in Q4, and 12.7% for the year, while property gross written premiums grew by 6.2% in Q4, and 6.4% for the year. The combined ratio for auto was 96.1% in Q4, and 96.7% for the year, while the property combined ratio was 82.8% in Q4 and 96.3% for the whole year.

news.headline: $50K in grants awarded to 2 Louisiana nonprofits by FHLB Dallas and Bonvenu Bank for resolving heirs’ property issues.
news.summary: In Baton Rouge, Louisiana, the Federal Home Loan Bank of Dallas (FHLB Dallas), in partnership with Bonvenu Bank, granted $50,000 to aid in resolving heirs’ property issues. The grants were awarded to two organizations - The BRidge Agency and EnvisioNBR - with each receiving $25,000. This funding aims to support low-income families in tackling heirs’ property title problems. The ceremonial check presentation took place recently.

news.headline: Allegion (NYSE: ALLE) to acquire Lemaar Australia Pty Ltd, expanding security portfolio; acquisition expected to close in Q1 2025.
news.summary: Allegion plc, a prominent global security products and solutions provider listed on the NYSE as ALLE, has announced the signing of a definitive agreement to purchase Lemaar Australia Pty Ltd, a Victoria-based door hardware company. Established in 1999, Lemaar has a strong reputation in Australia for its top-notch security and accessibility solutions. The acquisition, conducted through one of Allegion's subsidiaries, is projected to be finalized in the first quarter of 2025, pending the fulfillment of standard closing conditions.

news.summary: The founder of Binance, Changpeng Zhao, recently shared his dog's name in a social media post. This led to a surge in the creation of memecoins inspired by the canine, with numerous new tokens being named after it.

news.headline: Fairfax Financial Holdings Limited reported financial results for the year 2024 in U.S. dollars using IFRS standards, including underwriting profit, gross and net premiums written, combined ratio, float, and book value per share. Non-GAAP financial measures are included.
news.summary: The financial results disclosed in this news release are reported in U.S. dollars unless specified otherwise. These figures are based on unaudited consolidated financial statements conforming to the International Financial Reporting Standards set forth by the International Accounting Standards Board. It is important to note that certain financial measures included here, such as underwriting profit (loss), adjusted operating income (loss), gross premiums written, net premiums written, combined ratio (both discounted and undiscounted), float, book value per basic share, total debt to total capital ratio excluding non-insurance companies, and excess (deficiency) of fair value over carrying value, do not have an established meaning under IFRS Accounting Standards and may not be directly comparable to similar metrics used by other companies. For detailed explanations of these non-GAAP and other financial measurements, please refer to the "Glossary of non-GAAP and other financial measures" section provided at the conclusion of this release.

news.headline: MiniLuxe announces CFO transition: Brian Moran departs, former CFO Elizabeth Lorber steps in as Acting CFO.
news.summary: Following the announcement of the departure of the current CFO, Brian Moran, it has been disclosed that Elizabeth Lorber, the former CFO of MiniLuxe, will step in as the Acting CFO. Moran's exit from the Company has prompted this change in leadership. Elizabeth Lorber is set to take on the role temporarily until a permanent replacement is found. Moran's departure and Lorber's takeover signify a shift within the company's financial leadership. This move will see a transition in the CFO role after Moran's tenure. Elizabeth Lorber's experience in finance, gained during her time at MiniLuxe, makes her a suitable candidate for this interim position within the company. Moran's exit presents an opportunity for the company to reevaluate its financial strategies under new leadership. This announcement indicates a change in the company's financial direction and highlights the significance of finding a capable CFO to drive the financial agenda forward.

news.headline: MDU Resources Group appoints Darrel T. Anderson as vice chair of the board of directors, effective immediately.
news.summary: On February 13, 2025, in Bismarck, North Dakota, MDU Resources Group Inc. disclosed that Darrel T. Anderson has been appointed as the vice chair by the board of directors, with immediate effect. news.headline: Twilio's Q4 revenue slightly beats estimates at $1.19B, EPS falls short with $1.00. Active customer accounts grow to 325K.
news.summary: Twilio Inc (NYSE:TWLO) released its Q4 financial results, exceeding revenue estimates with $1.19 billion compared to the expected $1.183 billion. However, the EPS of $1.00 fell short of the estimated $1.04. The Q4 revenue saw an 11% increase year-over-year, with Communications revenue at $1.12 billion (up 12% YoY) and Segment revenue totaling $74.1 million (down 1% YoY). Twilio's active customer accounts rose to over 325,000 by Dec. 31 from 305,000 at the end of 2023. The company's Q4 operating cash flow was $108.4 million, with $93.5 million in free cash flow.

news.headline: Matinas BioPharma to sell 3,300 shares of Preferred Stock & warrants for $3.3 million to Investors, Dr. Robin L. Smith joins Board.
news.summary: Matinas BioPharma Holdings, Inc., based in Bedminster, New Jersey, announced on February 13, 2025, that they have entered into a securities purchase agreement with a group of investors. The agreement involves the purchase of 3,300 shares of Series C Convertible Preferred Stock and warrants to buy up to 11,262,808 shares of common stock. The purchase price for each share of Preferred Stock and accompanying warrants is $1,000, resulting in total gross proceeds of $3.3 million, excluding offering expenses to be covered by the Company. The Investors have agreed to these terms as per the Agreement.

news.headline: JEDMED and Otologic Technologies partner to launch world's first AI-enabled digital otoscope for ear diseases.
news.summary: A partnership has been formed between JEDMED Corporation, a leading provider of otoscopic solutions worldwide, and Otologic Technologies, the creator of an AI-driven diagnostic tool for ear conditions. The collaboration, announced on Feb. 13, 2025, aims to revolutionize ear disease diagnosis. JEDMED's extensive reach in the field combined with Otologic Technologies' innovative AI technology is expected to significantly enhance diagnostic capabilities. This groundbreaking partnership signifies a major advancement in the medical industry and is set to impact a large number of patients suffering from ear diseases globally.

news.headline: Philippa Newman promoted to Chief Brand and Product Officer at Capri Holdings Limited (NYSE:CPRI), overseeing all product and marketing functions.

Idol, Chairman and CEO of Capri Holdings and Michael Kors. With over 16 years of experience at Michael Kors, Ms. Newman held the position of Chief Product Officer before her latest promotion.

news.headline: Cohu Inc. (NASDAQ: COHU) Q4 2024: $94.1M net sales, $21.4M GAAP loss. Full year 2024: $401.8M sales, $69.8M loss. Non-GAAP: $7.1M Q4 loss.
news.summary: Cohu, Inc., a global provider of equipment and services for optimizing semiconductor manufacturing yield and productivity, announced its financial results for the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2024. The company reported net sales of $94.1 million for the quarter, with a GAAP loss of $21.4 million or $0.46 per share. For the full year 2024, net sales totaled $401.8 million, while the GAAP loss was $69.8 million or $1.49 per share. Additionally, Cohu reported non-GAAP results, showing a loss of $7.1 million or $0.15 per share for the fourth quarter of 2024.

news.headline: Motorola Solutions reported its Q4 and full-year 2024 earnings, with record sales, earning, and cash flow. CEO optimistic about continued growth.
news.summary: In Chicago, Motorola Solutions, Inc. (NYSE: MSI) disclosed its financial performance for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024. The CEO, Greg Brown, expressed the company's satisfaction with the results, highlighting record sales, operating earnings, and cash flow for the year. The strong demand for their safety and security solutions, along with a record backlog, sets a solid foundation for continued growth. news.headline: Verisign CFO George E. Kilguss III retires after 13 years; to retire on May 31, 2025; served as CFO since 2012.
news.summary: VeriSign, a company traded on NASDAQ under ticker symbol VRSN, specializes in providing essential internet infrastructure and domain registration services globally. The firm shared news of the retirement of George E. Kilguss III, who served as their Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer for 13 years. His retirement is set for May 31, 2025. In his role as CFO since 2012, Kilguss played a critical part in the company's financial operations. Following his retirement, it is expected that Kilguss will continue to support VeriSign in an advisory capacity.

news.headline: DraftKings surpasses profit expectations despite challenging sportsbook quarter. DKNG stock rises.
news.summary: A recent report indicates that the global economy is slowing down, with the growth rate dropping to 2.9% this year, the lowest in a decade. The trade tensions between the United States and China are cited as one of the primary reasons for this slowdown. The trade volume is expected to increase by only 1.2% this year, the weakest since the 2008 financial crisis. Additionally, global investment growth has fallen to 1%, the lowest since 2010. The International Monetary Fund has urged countries to resolve trade disputes and implement supportive policies to boost economic growth.
